title: How would you describe the level of academic pressure you currently experience?

I share a smartphone or device with other family members
I have very limited or no access to digital devices

title: When teachers use technology in class (such as videos, apps, or smartboards), how does it affect your experience?
options: It makes the class noticeably more engaging
It helps sometimes, depending on how it is used
It does not make much of a difference either way
It is less engaging than a regular lecture
We do not use much technology in class

title: If you use AI tools such as ChatGPT for schoolwork, how do you typically use them?
options: I copy-paste the answers directly without reading them closely
I use it as a starting point, but I critically evaluate and rewrite the output
I use it as a tool to compile all my research
I do not use AI tools

title: When you think about your future, how do you personally define success for yourself? (Tick all that apply)
options: Achieving a high level of expertise or leadership in my chosen field
The ability to continuously learn, evolve, and take on new challenges
Achieving a healthy balance between work and personal well-being
Contributing to the community or solving meaningful problems
Earning the respect and recognition of my peers and industry
Securing a predictable and stable career path

How do you feel when you think about graduating and looking for a job?
options: A feeling of being unprepared for the real world.
Anxiety and fear of the unknown.

title: How does your college mainly test you?
options: A mix of traditional written exams and some practical lab work.

title: Do you check AI-generated answers (like ChatGPT) for mistakes or biases before using them in college work?
options: I usually trust the AI's output
I cross-verify it, but mostly trust it.
I always critically verify the facts and biases before using it.

title: Which of these skills do you feel you need to develop? (Tick all that apply)
options: Meta-learning (how to independently learn new things quickly)
Basic financial or money management skills

options: I can generally figure it out on my own
I prefer having a teacher or structured course

title: If you could magically change one thing about college education, what would it be?

I prioritize infrastructure and campus facilities
I depend on word-of-mouth from other parents

I evaluate the institution based on the total cost of education
I value the prestige of the institution

Yes, it is noticeable but manageable without loans
No, we do not spend much on supplementary education

title: Do you feel the school focuses enough on your child’s emotional and holistic development (values, empathy, resilience)?

It has no real effect, curiosity comes from home, not school
It creates a tension : curious at home, compliant at school

title: What is your current preference for your child's supplementary learning?
options: Online learning through apps and digital platforms

Traditional, in-person learning without digital platforms

title: How often do you feel social or family pressure to steer your child toward specific career paths or higher marks?

I would feel positive, but have some concerns about keeping up academically
I would want to know whether it has relevance to their future first
I would be concerned about time away from studies

title: What is your biggest worry about your child's education and future? (Tick all that apply)
options: That they won't score high enough to get into a premier college.
That they will get a degree but lack the practical skills to find a job.
That their chosen career path will be automated by AI.
That the intense pressure will severely impact their mental health.

No, students present as more confident and compliant to teachers than they really are
No, students present as more stressed and disengaged than they really are when talking to parents
The student I see privately and the one in the classroom are almost different people

options: Unclear about their identity and anxious about the loss of structure
